question_text: A student observes that when a ball is dropped, it accelerates towards the ground. Identify the non-contact force responsible for this acceleration and state the direction in which it acts.
correct_answer: Gravitational force (or weight) is responsible for the acceleration. It acts vertically downwards towards the centre of the Earth.
explanation: Gravitational force is a non-contact force that pulls all objects with mass towards the Earth. It doesn't need the objects to be touching—it works from a distance. This is why dropped balls fall downwards; gravity is pulling them towards the centre of the Earth.
